rqs: What are the time and space complexities of QuickSort, Merge Sort, Radix Sort, Parallel Sorting, and GPU Sorting compared to Bubble, Insertion, Heap, and Selection Sort in academic journals?
Which sorting algorithms achieve the best execution time and scalability on high‑performance computing systems versus traditional sorts in benchmark suites?
In which contexts (open source repositories, industry reports) are parallel and GPU sorting methods adopted over classic algorithms, and what memory usage benefits are reported?
base_query: ("Algorithm Researchers" OR "Software Developers" OR "Database Systems" OR "Embedded Systems" OR "High Performance Computing") AND ("QuickSort" OR "Merge Sort" OR "Radix Sort" OR "Parallel Sorting" OR "GPU Sorting") AND ("Bubble Sort" OR "Insertion Sort" OR "Heap Sort" OR "Selection Sort") AND ("Time Complexity" OR "Space Complexity" OR "Execution Time" OR "Memory Usage" OR "Scalability") AND ("Academic Journals" OR "Conference Proceedings" OR "Open Source Repositories" OR "Benchmark Suites" OR "Industry Reports")
arxiv_query: (all:"Algorithm Researchers" OR all:"Software Developers" OR all:"Database Systems" OR all:"Embedded Systems" OR all:"High Performance Computing") AND (all:"QuickSort" OR all:"Merge Sort" OR all:"Radix Sort" OR all:"Parallel Sorting" OR all:"GPU Sorting") AND (all:"Bubble Sort" OR all:"Insertion Sort" OR all:"Heap Sort" OR all:"Selection Sort") AND (all:"Time Complexity" OR all:"Space Complexity" OR all:"Execution Time" OR all:"Memory Usage" OR all:"Scalability") AND (all:"Academic Journals" OR all:"Conference Proceedings" OR all:"Open Source Repositories" OR all:"Benchmark Suites" OR all:"Industry Reports")
